name: ECE B.S.
description: BS in Electrical and Computer Engineering — 379 units. Core, Area, Coverage, Capstone, General Technical, and Free Elective requirements.

categories.name: ECE Core
categories.description: 61 units of required ECE core courses.

categories.name: Area: Device Sci & Nanofab
categories.description: Solid State Physics, Semiconductors, Electromagnetics, Optics, etc.
categories.notes: Two courses (24 units) from one area + one course (12 units) from a second area required.

categories.name: Area: Signals & Systems
categories.description: DSP, Communications, Control, Power Systems, etc.
categories.notes: Two courses (24 units) from one area + one course (12 units) from a second area required.

categories.name: Area: Circuits
categories.description: Analog and Digital Circuits, IC Design, etc.
categories.notes: Two courses (24 units) from one area + one course (12 units) from a second area required.

categories.name: Area: Hardware Systems
categories.notes: Two courses (24 units) from one area + one course (12 units) from a second area required.

categories.name: Area: Software Systems
categories.description: Embedded Systems, Networks, OS, Compilers, Security, etc.
categories.notes: Two courses (24 units) from one area + one course (12 units) from a second area required.

categories.name: Coverage
categories.description: 12 units to further specialize or explore other ECE areas. All ECE 18-3XX and above qualify, plus select non-ECE courses.
categories.notes: Any ECE course 18-300+ qualifies. Listed non-ECE courses also approved. Undergraduate research (18-580, 39-500) can also fulfill this.

categories.name: Capstone Design
categories.description: 12 units — hands-on team engineering design experience.
categories.notes: One course required. Prerequisites: 18-220, 18-290, 18-240, 18-213, plus two ECE Area courses.

categories.name: General Technical
categories.description: 63 units including math, physics, probability, and programming prerequisites.
categories.notes: Includes calculus (21-120, 21-122), physics (33-141, 33-142), probability (21-325 or 36-219 or 36-225), programming (15-112, 15-122), math foundations (18-202), plus two math/science electives from MCS or Statistics (excluding certain courses).
